---
title: "Overview of design for new nimbleModel"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

To more efficiently process nimbleModels and provide for scalability, we are redesigning key aspects of nimbleModel defining and building. The key pieces of this are:

- representing values of individual indexes (and groups of indexes when paired in arbitrary ways) of a variable as abstractions called varRanges rather than (unless required) complete unrolling
- providing functionality to determine graph dependencies abstractly based on the index abstraction, including child and parent relationships, using what we call index inversion
- representing the relationship between variable indexes and nodeFunction code, such as `y[i+1] <- x[i]`
- providing topological sorting at the declaration level that allows for ties
- determining what nodeFunction calculations need to be done based on the variables and their indexes provided by a user or by a dependency calculation
- providing multiple versions of nodeFunctions so as to allow vectorized calculations, and determining which version to call
- providing efficient (sparse in some form) storage of variable information, including topological order, isData status, node type, etc.
